prompt: Guarda il codice. Quali sono i 3 bug più probabili che un utente incontrerà nel primo mese di uso di ?
content:
1. `hewn-mcp` MCP server fails under pipx install
   evidence: `integrations/claude-code/mcp-config.json:4` uses `"command": "python3"`; `install.sh:121` prefers `pipx install`
   trigger/impact: pipx isolates `flint` module in its own venv; system `python3` cannot import `flint.mcp_server`. User launches `hewn-mcp`, Claude Code spawns MCP server, `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'flint'`, `submit_flint_ir` tool never appears in session. Silent — only `--debug` surfaces it.
   fix: replace `python3` with pipx-aware resolution (`pipx run --spec flint-ir python -m flint.mcp_server`), or rewrite installer to emit an absolute interpreter path in `mcp-config.json` at install time.

2. no `/hewn*` slash commands despite Hewn rebrand
   evidence: `install.sh:22` `SKILLS=(flint flint-on flint-off flint-audit)`; `README.md:1-5` brands product as "Hewn"; `integrations/claude-code/skills/` only has `flint*` dirs
   trigger/impact: user reads Hewn-branded README, types `/hewn`, `/hewn-on`, `/hewn-audit` in Claude Code — treated as literal text, no skill fires, no error. Most new users assume product broken.
   fix: add `hewn`, `hewn-on`, `hewn-off`, `hewn-audit` skill dirs (copies or symlinks of `flint*`), extend `SKILLS` array, update README/installer banner to list both.

3. silent install failure leaves `flint-ir` / `hewn-ir` CLI absent on PEP 668 systems
   evidence: `install.sh:121-126` runs `pipx install ... || pipx install --force ... || true` then fallback `pip install --user ... || true`; every failure mode swallowed; `echo "✓ Hewn installed."` prints unconditionally
   trigger/impact: modern Debian/Ubuntu without pipx + PEP 668 externally-managed python → `pip install --user` errors, swallowed. Installer reports success. `/flint-audit` skill calls `flint-ir audit --explain` → command not found. `hewn-mcp` also broken. User sees cryptic "command not found" only when using feature.
   fix: drop `|| true` around the install step, detect failure and print explicit remediation (`install pipx` or `pip install --user --break-system-packages`); exit non-zero so banner is not misleading.

input.command: which pipx python3 claude 2>&1; pipx --version 2>/dev/null; pipx list 2>/dev/null | head -20
